L1.area: Data serialization and wire protocol handling layer

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the binary data encoding and decoding routines used for client-server protocol communication. A malicious server can send crafted responses that cause an integer overflow due to the use of insufficiently sized signed integer types in the variable-length integer parsing logic, potentially leading to a crash or arbitrary code execution on the client.

L3.area: Variable-length integer encoding and decoding routines in the wire protocol serialization layer
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the variable-length integer (LEB128) encoding and decoding routines used for ClickHouse client-server protocol communication. A malicious ClickHouse server can send crafted responses that cause signed integer overflow due to the use of platform-dependent signed integer types instead of unsigned 64-bit types, potentially leading to a crash or arbitrary code execution on the client.

ground_truth.reason: The variable-length integer read and write functions use Py_ssize_t (a signed, platform-dependent type) for accumulating results and shifts, allowing a malicious server to trigger an integer overflow that leads to a buffer overflow and potential arbitrary code execution on the client.
